item.title: FTC and KCC Reach Agreement for 2 Grace Hill, Folkestone
item.description: Folkestone Town Council are delighted to confirm that agreement has been reached, subject to contract, to acquire 2 Grace Hill, Folkestone – securing the long-term future of this much-loved building as a community asset. After Kent County Council confirmed early in 2026 that it intended to dispose o…

item.title: Next talk: 25 April – George Fox: Founder of the Quakers by Jean Hatton
item.description: For full details and to book, see the event page. The post Next talk: 25 April – George Fox: Founder of the Quakers by Jean Hatton first appeared on New Folkestone Society.

item.title: Coastal Park play time gets a step closer
item.description: Work to deliver an exciting new era for the Lower Leas Coastal Park play area is about to get underway.

item.title: Helping to fund Road of Remembrance repair
item.description: Folkestone & Hythe District Council is to contribute £236,000 towards the work required to reopen the Road of Remembrance.

item.title: Folkestone Museum Receives Funding from the Royal Society
item.description: Folkestone Museum has been awarded funding by the Royal Society in the latest round of its Places of Science scheme. Twenty-seven small museums across the UK will use this funding to engage communities with their local science stories.The Places of Science scheme celebrate projects that evoke curios…

item.title: Updated CPZ framework agreed by cabinet
item.description: A joined-up approach to the implementation of Controlled Parking Zones (CPZs) has been approved.

item.title: Supporting Folkestone Sports Centre reopening
item.description: A £2.4m grant which will enable the reopening of swimming facilities at Folkestone Sports Centre has been supported.

item.title: The Town Mayor’s Community Awards 2025/26
item.description: Every year, around March, the Town Mayor of Folkestone has the privilege of awarding local people a Town Mayor’s Community Award. Previous recipients of this award have varied from volunteers of a local church to people working to support those most in need. This year, it was the turn of Councillor …

item.title: Otterpool Park collaboration agreement renewed
item.description: A new planning collaboration agreement has been signed aimed at supporting and accelerating the delivery of Otterpool Park Garden Town.

item.title: Anti-litter poster competition launched
item.description: Folkestone & Hythe District Council is calling on young artists to help champion a cleaner, greener environment by creating eye‑catching anti‑litter posters.

item.title: Folkestone ANPR trial comes to an end
item.description: The Sandgate Road car park in Folkestone will return to a traditional pay and display system following feedback on a trial using cameras.

item.title: Working together for our brave veterans
item.description: Charities and organisations have pledged to further improve support for military veterans and active personnel at an event organised by Folkestone & Hythe District Council.

item.title: Coffee Afternoon – Ministry of Sewage – Mermaid Beach
item.description: For full details, see the event page. The post Coffee Afternoon – Ministry of Sewage – Mermaid Beach first appeared on New Folkestone Society.

item.title: Beach management work gets underway again
item.description: A beach management programme which successfully defended homes from Storm Goretti in January will resume next week.

item.title: Giving a historic building an exciting future
item.description: An important step towards bringing an iconic Folkestone building back into public use is to be made next week.

item.title: Folkestone is a Library – A Town-Wide Celebration of Stories (and a World Record Book Swap!)
item.description: Folkestone has always been a place of creativity, connection and community – and now, we’re turning that spirit into something truly historic. This spring, the town comes alive with Folkestone is a Library, a bold, inclusive project that reimagines what a library can be… and brings everyone to…

item.title: Folkestone Victoria Pier: A Story of Ambition, Entertainment and Seaside Heritage
item.description: Folkestone has long been defined by its relationship with the sea – from the elegance of the Leas to the energy of its shoreline. Among its most ambitious Victorian projects was the Victoria Pier, a place that brought together engineering, entertainment, and the social life of the town. Before…

item.title: Folkestone's brighter future is halfway there
item.description: A £22m project to regenerate Folkestone town centre is now more than halfway complete.

item.title: Looking forward with balanced 2026/27 budget
item.description: Folkestone & Hythe District Council’s 2026/27 budget – the third in succession not to be funded from general reserves – has been approved.

item.title: First preview of new Coastal Park play area
item.description: Folkestone’s Lower Leas Coastal Park play area has delighted two generations of children over the past quarter of a century.

item.title: Welcome for more freight to go on trains
item.description: Investment enabling thousands of tonnes of freight heading across the Channel to be carried by rail again has been welcomed by Folkestone & Hythe District Council.

item.title: 25 Years of Go Folkestone: A Community Celebration
item.description: This summer, Go Folkestone turns 25, and we’re marking the moment the best way we know how: by bringing people together to celebrate everything that makes this town special. The Go Folkestone Anniversary Festival is more than a birthday party. It’s a day shaped by the people, places, and stories tha…

item.title: Delivering affordable green homes at Risborough
item.description: Folkestone & Hythe District Council is celebrating a major milestone as the first council tenants move into new affordable rent homes at the Risborough Barracks development.

item.title: WILD LEEKS – STOP THE INVASION
item.description: Wild Leeks are an invasive species that are smothering many of our gardens and public spaces. Here’s what you should know. .fusion-body .fusion-builder-column-0{width:100% !important;margin-top : 0px;margin-bottom : 20px;}.fusion-builder-column-0 > .fusion-column-wrapper {padding-top : 0px !im…

item.title: Join workshops to support Folkestone's town of culture bid
item.description: Bringing together the local community to help tell the rich and unique cultural story of Folkestone is the focus of open events being held in the town.

item.title: Next Talk: 28 February – Islamesque by Diana Darke
item.description: For full details, see event page. The post Next Talk: 28 February – Islamesque by Diana Darke first appeared on New Folkestone Society.

item.title: Getting parking policy right in the district
item.description: Feedback on a new joined-up approach to the implementation of Controlled Parking Zones (CPZs) across the district is being invited.

item.title: Scheme supporting those who need it most
item.description: An improved council tax reduction scheme which will further help working age, low-income households will come into effect in April.

item.title: Are You Proud of Folkestone? Have your say!
item.description: For full details, see our Coffee Afternoon page. The post Are You Proud of Folkestone? Have your say! first appeared on New Folkestone Society.

item.title: Armed Forces Day 2026!
item.description: ARMED FORCES DAY FOLKESTONE 2026 – FREE FAMILY FUN DAY WITH AIR DISPLAYS on Saturday 27th June 10am – 6pm THE LEAS BANDSTAND, ARENA AREA & THE LEAS, CT20 2XL   Folkestone Town Council are proud and delighted to announce that its 14th annual Armed Forces & Community Fun Day will i…

item.title: Armed Forces Day 2026 Sponsorship Opportunity
item.description: Armed Forces Day in Folkestone, is now in its 14th year and is one of the largest one day shows in the area attracting more than 50,000 visitors. Taking place on Saturday 27 June 2026, Folkestone Armed Forces Day prides itself in showcasing air displays alongside a stage for local talent whil…

item.title: Have your say on local government reorganisation
item.description: Government ministers have launched a consultation on the future number of councils in Kent and Medway and the areas they should cover.

item.title: Council commitment to affordable homes continues
item.description: Six new homes are to be provided by the council in the rural village of Lyminge as part of a pledge to create more affordable, energy efficient homes in the district.

item.title: 2026 Newsletter
item.description: Here’s our 2026 Newsletter which gives a run down of what we have been doing in 2025, and our plans for the rest of the year. ENJOY!! .fusion-body .fusion-builder-column-1{width:100% !important;margin-top : 0px;margin-bottom : 20px;}.fusion-builder-column-1 > .fusion-column-wrapper {padding-to…

item.title: Folkestone Remembers Holocaust Memorial Day 2026
item.description: On Tuesday 27 January 2026, a commemoration service was held in the Town of Folkestone to mark the annual Holocaust Memorial Day, an internationally recognised day to remember the millions of people who were murdered or whose lives were changed beyond recognition during the Holocaust, Nazi persecuti…

item.title: FTC Approved Budget 2026/2027
item.description: Folkestone Town Council (FTC) is committed to improving and enhancing the Town of Folkestone in an environmentally sustainable manner, having regard to the identified needs and wishes of all sections of the community, whilst delivering the Town Council’s vision ‘To revitalise our town of Folkestone,…

item.title: FTC Potential Purchase of the Grace Hill Building
item.description: Following a resolution by Folkestone Town Council at their meeting on 22 January 2026, a letter has been sent to Kent County Council setting out the strong local community interest in the potential purchase of the freehold of the Grace Hill Library building for a nominal sum.   Th…

item.title: Spectacular Anglo-Saxon Sword to go on display at Folkestone Museum
item.description: Finds from a top-secret Anglo-Saxon cemetery in East Kent, including a complete sixth century sword with silver-gilt hilt fittings, will go on display at Folkestone Museum in July 2026. This sword was featured on Digging for Britain last year. It is one of the finest Anglo-Saxon swords ever found in…

item.title: January talk: History of the Clifton Hotel
item.description: Visit the event page for full details and to book The post January talk: History of the Clifton Hotel first appeared on New Folkestone Society.

item.title: Mindful Messy Making 2026
item.description: Following the success of last years taster sessions we are delighted to be running another series of creative peer support sessions facilitated by Sarah Lloyd. I found I could say things with colour and shapes that I couldn’t say any other way – things I had no words for.” – …

item.title: Past Events + Exhibitions
item.description: Sick Of It: The Egg-Xhibition & Trail Studio Sale Tangled Christmas-Not-Christmas Design + Art Fair 2025 Fourth Wall’s Fourth Birthday Party Still Sick Of It Riots Not Diets Returns Still out of CONtext Every Body: A Community Life Drawing Celebration Ageing Without Children Embodied The B…

item.title: The Town Mayor’s Christmas Message 2025
item.description: Firstly, I would love to say Merry Christmas and a Happy New Year to you all. What a year 2025 has been. Whether you’ve had an amazing year or a more challenging one, as it draws to an end let us all take time to unwind and reflect. For me Christmas is about the food, the festivities, s…

item.title: The Town Mayor’s Year 6 Christmas Carol Concert 2025
item.description: Each year the Town Mayor of Folkestone holds a Christmas Carol Concert for all Year Six Primary School children within Folkestone, held at the St Mary & St Eanswythe Church, The Bayle. This year, the Town Mayor, Cllr Lucy McGirr was delighted to welcome almost 300 children from nine schools with…

item.title: In 2025, Gabriela Golder’s ‘Conversation Piece’ has an urgent message
item.description: In 2025, Gabriela Golder’s ‘Conversation Piece’ has an urgent message Eleanor Peake # Eleanor Peake reflects on Gabriela Golder’s ‘Conversation Piece’. She asks, in a world of YouTube radicalisation and political isolation, what would happen if the far right spoke to their grandmothers? Three genera…

item.title: The Town Mayor’s Christmas Card Competition 2025
item.description: The Worshipful Town Mayor of Folkestone, Councillor Lucy McGirr, invited students in year groups 7 and 8 at local secondary schools to design a picture reflecting ‘Festive Folkestone’ for this year’s annual Christmas card, which will be sent out to local businesses and dignitaries, including His Maj…

item.title: Watching ‘O Espírito da TV’
item.description: Watching ‘O Espírito da TV’ Daisy Whittle # In the first season of REEL BRAZIL: Mutinies in Video Art, the film O Espírito da TV spotlights the Waiãpi people of the Amazon Rainforest. It shows their bold fight to hold onto their land and traditions against the push of new technology and …

item.title: November Talk: 29th Folkestone Fragments
item.description: Visit the Folkestone Fragments Talk Infomation Page for more details. The post November Talk: 29th Folkestone Fragments first appeared on New Folkestone Society.

item.title: The BIG Sleep Out – Your Way
item.description: Brave the BIG Sleep Out – Your Way and raise money for Rainbow Centre’s homeless work Our BIG Sleep Out season runs from 1 December 25 until 28 February 26 alongside our Folkestone Churches Winter Shelter service, fundraising for our homeless work. What is the BIG Sleep Out – Your …

item.title: Launch of Crowdfunder
item.description: The Board of Trustees of the Romney Marsh Community Hub charity, which this year marks its 50th anniversary serving the Marsh, has launched an urgent fundraising appeal to raise £150,000 to safeguard essential services for local residents. The charity has been hit hard by a combination of soaring op…

item.title: Matthew’s Volunteering Journey
item.description: published 18/11/25 Article Update Since starting volunteering with us, Matthew has gone from strength to strength and we are so pleased to say he has now secured permanent employment, and he is continuing to volunteer for us too! Huge congratulations Matthew, the Warehouse Team and everyone at Rainb…

item.title: State and marginalisation: Brazil and the UK 1970-1990
item.description: State and marginalisation: Brazil and the UK 1970-1990 Magdalena Araus Sieber # The Brazilian civil-military dictatorship (1964-1985) and Margaret Thatcher’s government in the UK (1979-1990) overlapped for several years. Despite crucial differences, most notably that Thatcher governed within a democ…

item.title: Queer resistance during Brazil’s dictatorship
item.description: Carnival of 1984, Salvador. Luiz Mott and members of the Grupo Gay da Bahia. Source: Grupo Gay Da Bahia ‘Lampião da Esquina’ one of the first publications directed at the homosexual public in Brazil, 1978. Source: Wikimedia Commons Protest against the police patrols, 1980 James Green Collection. Sou…

item.title: November Coffee Afternoon
item.description: Visit the coffee afternoon page for more details. The post November Coffee Afternoon first appeared on New Folkestone Society.

item.title: Sick Of It: The Egg-Xhibition
item.description: We’re doing things a little differently this year. After moving out of our space on the Old High Street we’re kicking this new journey off by combining our well loved Sick Of It festival with our fundraising Egg-Xhibition. We have invited a wonderful group of artists to take part in creating a trail…

item.title: Open Call
item.description: SICK OF IT EGG-XHIBITION OPEN CALLFRI 27 MAR – SUN 12 APRThis year, for our very first Fourth Wall Folkestone pop up – we’re combining our annual ‘Sick of It’ festival with our fundraising ‘Egg-xhibition’.The Sick Of It Egg-xhibition will encompass all of Fourth Wall Folkestone’s core in…

item.title: Studio Sale
item.description: We’re leaving our current space and we can’t take everything with us! Pop along to our studio sale to grab a bargain or two! Open 12-4pm on the following dates (or until we run out of things to sell!):Thu 22 JanFri 23 JanSat 24 JanSun 25 JanWed 28 JanThu 29 JanFri 30 JanSat 31 JanSun 01 …

item.title: Tangled
item.description: ALBA SHANKLAND AMETI + EMMA SHANKLANDThu 08 Jan – Sun 18 Jan Exhibition Open:Thu-Sun, 12:00-16:00 ABOUT THE EXHIBITION Tangled: a mixed media exhibition from mother and daughter Emma Shankland and Alba Shankland Ameti explores growing up—and growing into yourself—when life feels unclear and un…

item.title: Christmas Not Christmas Design + Art Fair 2025
item.description: Fri 14 Nov – Wed 24 DecOpen: Thu-Sun, 12:00-17:00Plus: Mon 22 & Tue 23 Dec 12:00-17:00 and Christmas Eve 10:30-14:00 Our Christmas-Not-Christmas Design + Art Fair 2025 focuses on objects that are both beautiful and functional. It is a celebration of creativity in Kent and champions shoppin…

item.title: Remembrance 2025
item.description: More information on this page The post Remembrance 2025 first appeared on New Folkestone Society.

item.title: Talk: Samuel Plimsoll
item.description: Saturday 25th October 2pm for 2:30 start. Location: The Cornerstone Centre For more details, see the event page The post Talk: Samuel Plimsoll first appeared on New Folkestone Society.

item.title: Coffee Afternoon – Legislative Theatre
item.description: The next coffee afternoon will be on 9 October – Legislative Theatre with Matthew Hahn of FPAC. Join us at The Community Room on The Bayle to learn more, and register your interest. As part of Folkestone Fringe: Flint and Clay 2025, FPAC will be inviting local residents to co-create a short piece of…

item.title: How to Get Involved in Folkestone’s Future (Even if You’re Busy)
item.description: We all want to live in a town that feels welcoming, inspiring, and cared for. But for many of us, the thought of “getting involved” feels daunting. Who has hours of spare time these days? The good news is that supporting Folkestone’s future doesn’t require huge amounts of time. With Go Folkestone, t…

item.title: Why Local History Still Matters in a Modern Town
item.description: We live in a fast-paced world. Towns change, skylines shift, and new businesses come and go. It can be tempting to think heritage is something we leave in museums or history books. But in reality, local history is what gives a place its character – and without it, we risk losing not only our i…

item.title: Small Acts, Big Change: How Community Projects Shape a Town
item.description: When you picture the kind of projects that change a town, you might think of large-scale developments or huge investments. But often, the most lasting impact comes from the small, consistent actions of people who care. That’s the heart of what Go Folkestone has been doing for more than 20 years &#82…

item.title: Introduction to Large Format Cameras
item.description: Step into the world of large format photography! We’ll cover the essentials—from camera setup and proper film loading and handling, to mastering light metering for accurate exposures. We’ll also explore advanced camera movements like tilt, shift, and swing—powerful tools for creative control a…

item.title: b&w Film Developing
item.description: Take creative control over your photography by learning how to develop black & white film at home. From contrast to grain, home development lets you shape your images exactly how you envision them. Our hands-on class is perfect for beginners and enthusiasts alike – no prior experience need…

item.title: Been out with the Cambo
item.description: decided to give farge format photography a try since I read so much about it. It’s quite a steep learning curve with room for improvement but practice makes perfect.

item.title: Developed my first film!
item.description: Always wanted to do that but the cost of buying all the quipment put me off. With Folkeshare I can hire the bits I need at very low cost and don’t have to store more stuff. They include everything needed apart from the chemistry. For that we have 2 camera shops near by.

item.title: Shuffler Launch at Our Hub
item.description: We are proud to be one of only three sites in Kent chosen to launch and test the SHUFFLER® — a new portable seated exercise device. The SHUFFLER® was co-designed by an NHS doctor to help improve mobility and stability in a simple, hassle-free way. It is suitable for people of all abilities — includi…

item.title: Fantastic Funday Fundraiser
item.description: Lindau Residential Home in New Romney held a funday on Saturday 9 August. It was a busy and happy day. There was live music from Driss, a BBQ, a cake stall, face painting, line dancing, and the biggest raffle we have ever seen. The sun was shining. Residents enjoyed the day with their family and fri…

item.title: David’s 80th Birthday Bucket List Item Takes Off!
item.description: Our amazing volunteer, David Stokes, decided to celebrate his upcoming 80th birthday in the most adventurous way possible — by doing a wing walk! On Friday 1 August, David climbed onto the top of a plane at Headcorn Aerodrome and soared through the skies, ticking off a top item from his bucket list.…

item.title: Go Folkestone July NEWS 2025
item.description: Dear Go Folkestone Member, Attached please find Go Folkestone July 2025 News. Kind regards Nicola Tolson Membership Secretary

item.title: Latest News From The Leas Lift
item.description: Dear Go Folkestone Member, You may be interested to read the latest update on The Leas Lift. Nicola Tolson Membership Secretary

item.title: Go Folkestone Litter Pick – Friday 25th July 2.00pm
item.description: Dear Go Folkestone Member We would love you to join us at The Leas Arch this Friday, 25th July at 2.00pm to take part in our monthly Litter Pick. Patty Key, our Litter Pick Leader, will be with Gary from FHDC and his white van to hand out the tools required to collect litter – i.e. gloves, pickers, …

item.title: General Meeting Minutes July 2025
item.description: Dear Go Folkestone Member Attached please find Minutes from our July meeting. We would love to see you at the next meeting on 13th August at The Burlington Hotel, Earls Avenue. 6.30 for 7.00pm start. Correction With apologies: Electrical Waste Recycling – Figures were incorrect in the July Min…

item.title: Historic Annual Meeting highlights need for supported accommodation in Folkestone
item.description: On Friday, 4th July 2025, our charity held a special Annual Meeting, marking forty years since the Rainbow Centre was founded. Key figures from the organisation’s history shared their reflections on the charity’s past, while inspiring talks from Emeritus Professor Peter Taylor-Gooby (OBE…

item.title: HOW I DESIGNED THE DAM IN VENICE – #1
item.description: HOW I DESIGNED THE DAM IN VENICE – #1 Artist blog series by Nina Shen # In early 2023, CT20’s director Nina Shen was invited by the activist-curator David Hevey to work out how to put on a show in Venice – CRIP ARTE SPAZIO: The DAM in Venice – which showcases for the first time, on…

item.title: Ellis’s story
item.description: Homelessness can happen to anyone. Meet Ellis*. At 42 years old he became homeless, living in a tent and unable to afford even basic necessities. Ellis is a skilled carpenter and plasterer. He was in a relationship and had full-time employment. But when he and his partner split up, he turned to alco…

item.title: The Sports Trust Saves Folkestone Sports Centre
item.description: The Sports Trust is delighted to announce that it has exchanged contracts to acquire the Folkestone Sports Centre. After many months of productive discussions with the Administrator and key stakeholders, it has finalised an agreement and anticipates taking ownership shortly. Dan Hulme, Chief Executi…
item.organisation: The Roger De Haan Charitable Trust

item.title: App (v1.0.0) Launched – We need your feedback!
item.description: Our app is now in open beta phase, and we’d love your feedback! Please complete the form below if you have anything you wish to share. As a new platform in its infancy, we are aware of a small number of issues and are working hard to resolve these as quickly as possible. If you […] The post App (v1.…

item.title: Being a Trustee
item.description: by Marion Howard-Healy How did that happen?Having some spare time, I wondered how I could volunteer locally. What to do? The Romney Marsh Community Hub came to mind. After an initial inquiry, I was directed to the website and saw the Hub was looking for Trustees. Thinking my business skills might be…

item.title: Hamlet Hotel Planning Application Accepted
item.description: 19th March 2025 The Rainbow Centre is pleased to announce that yesterday, Folkestone and Hythe District Council, gave the green light for the conversion of a Folkestone hotel into accommodation for local people experiencing homelessness. Dr Peter Le Feuvre, the Chair of Trustees, confirmed that the …

item.title: Musical Walkabout
item.description: The Musical Walkabout were able to run its Coastal Project through the summer of 2024 with the help of funds from RDHCT. The Walkabout works with people with dementia, their carers and health and social care professionals to enjoy music as a weekly event. The group sing songs in the Lower Leas Coast…
item.organisation: The Roger De Haan Charitable Trust

item.title: 40 Challenges Fundraiser
item.description: This year the Rainbow Centre marks 40 years supporting the most vulnerable in our community. We want to raise 40K to help fund our vital services. Can you, or someone you know, help by taking one of our fun 40 Challenges? These fundraising activities are for individuals, the whole family or groups o…

item.title: Volunteering Opportunities
item.description: Do You have some time to give back to your community? New Volunteering Opportunities We are pleased to announce that we have 2 new volunteering opportunities:Hub On the Beach – we need volunteers to help in the café Monday – Sunday, there are 2 shifts, 09.30 – 13.30 or 12.00 &#8211…

item.title: Launch of Hub on the beach
item.description: We Officially Launch and Open the Hub On The Beach On Monday 2nd of September we officially launched our latest project, the Hub On The Beach.  Our longest standing volunteer, Margaret Edwards, did the honours of cutting the ribbon and declaring the Hub open. Entertainment was provided by the E…

item.title: The Marsh Bee is here
item.description: We are really pleased to announce the Launch of our Dial a Ride Service We are really pleased to be able to launch our newest initiative today, The Marsh Bee. This is a membership dial a ride service for people living on the Romney Marsh to access medical appointments at their GP surgeries or local …

item.title: SFF 2023
item.description: Connect – Be Inspired – Collaborate; to create a more sustainable futures for our district. On Sat 29th April 2023 the Folkestone & Hythe Sustainable Futures Forum bought together businesses, community groups and individuals to deliver inspiring talks and ideas from local professiona…

item.title: Fourth Wall Birthday Party
item.description: Join us to celebrate Fourth Wall Folkestone’s Fourth Birthday! On Friday 28 November, between 5-8pm, we’ll be opening up our Christmas-Not-Christmas Design + Art Fair late and cracking out the bubbles to mark our 4th anniversary. We’d love for you to join us! We’ve also annou…
